chunk_map.heading: Two windows on one codebase (H1 + lede)
Fork the side worktree from your security lane
Open the quality lane in the new worktree
Set the two windows side by side

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Referent resolution under a sequential deck read.
rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 3 "Open the quality lane in the new worktree": line 56: "You author the test-strategy skill here while the main lane runs security." — "the test-strategy skill" is never named anywhere earlier in this file; it is introduced in the module file's own What-You'll-Learn/Key-Concepts sections (earn-the-trust.md), not in this exercise, and it hasn't been authored yet at this point in the story (authoring happens later, at Exercise 3). No same-file antecedent exists to resolve the definite noun.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Either unpack the term in-sentence on first use here (name what it is and why it matters) or defer the mention to the exercise/lecture where it's actually introduced.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every backward reference points at a beat that exists.
Live-demo beats survive a text read.

rules_evaluated.evidence: No "Not X. Not Y. Z." constructions, anaphora/epistrophe triples, self-grading superlatives, number-with-retraction, abstraction-noun stacks, or empty-leg verb triplets found in the body across all chunks.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Internal dialect never lands on a slide.
